—The people acted corruptly. Perhaps Jotham was lacking in his efforts to reform the land. People can be very good, but still lack the courage and zeal to do what they could. It certainly places blame on the people. Jotham prospered and became powerful. The more steadfast we are in religion, the more powerful we are, both to resist evil and to do good. The Lord often removes wise and pious rulers and sends others, whose follies and vices punish a people who did not value their blessings.
